_comment: Baked dogfood-marathon REPORT deck — deterministic fallback used by no-LLM flows. Live runs overwrite this from journaled run data via scripts/build_deck.star. The structure mirrors the real generator: title -> objective status -> scoreboard cards -> before/after affordance -> outcome table -> evidence.

scenes.title: Backlog → pipeline → report
scenes.subtitle: Before / after · result status · evidence · cost
scenes.narration: A queue of cases is driven through the configured inner workflow, independently verified, then reported with scan-friendly before-after and result affordances.

scenes.title: Run status at a glance

items.label: Solved
items.detail: ✅ independently verified solved cases are green.

items.label: Needs review
items.detail: ⚠ partial or needs-human cases stay orange and visible.

items.label: Failed
items.detail: ❌ failed oracle checks are red and never buried in prose.

items.label: Evidence decked
items.detail: Every recorded case gets a focused per-bug deck plus the aggregate table.

scenes.caption: Green check = solved. Orange warning = operator review. Red exclamation = failed verification.

scenes.title: Outcome scoreboard
scenes.caption: The scoreboard is designed to survive a sped-up recording: icon, color, short label, then detail.

scenes.title: How each bug deck reads
scenes.caption: The same before/after affordance appears in every per-bug deck.

scenes.title: Aggregate outcome table
scenes.caption: Concise scan view. Full source, trace, worktree, and fix evidence lives in each per-bug deck.

scenes.title: Issues and findings stay visible

items.label: Kitsoku Issue #61
items.detail: Operator scope question is visible as a warning, not a fake confirmation.

items.label: case trace
items.detail: Trace links are explicit evidence rows.

items.label: finding
items.detail: Friction feeds generic hardening work, not one-off overfitting.

scenes.caption: Warnings, traces, and findings get their own glyphs and references.
